key_metrics: Provision for credit losses: $384 million (up 36.2% YoY)
qoq_changes: Net revenues decreased 3.2% sequentially to $14.58 billion (vs estimated $15.06 billion in Q1 2025 based on YTD figures)
Net earnings decreased 21.4% sequentially to $3.72 billion (vs estimated $4.74 billion in Q1 2025 based on YTD figures)
new_risks: Provision for credit losses increased 36.2% YoY to $384 million, indicating potential credit deterioration or portfolio stress
Total operating expenses increased 8.3% YoY to $9.24 billion, driven by higher compensation and transaction-based costs
management_tone: Bullish but prudent; strong YoY growth in core investment banking and market making highlights franchise strength, though higher credit provisions reflect a cautious stance on the macro environment.
analyst_note: Goldman Sachs posted a robust Q2 2025, driven by a strong rebound in investment banking and steady market making revenues. While sequential results cooled slightly from a massive Q1, the 26%+ YoY growth in EPS demonstrates excellent operating leverage despite rising credit provisions.
